text.title: cost-estimation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cost-estimation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Salary equals total cost — applying no overhead multiplier to labor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Employer taxes, benefits, and facilities add 30-50% above salary. Omitting them produces a baseline that is structurally underfunded from day one.

failure-mode.description: Flat contingency percentage applied without a risk register.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 15% on a $500K project with three high-probability risks is likely insufficient; 15% on a low-risk maintenance project is padding. Risk-driven reserves calibrate to actual exposure.

failure-mode.description: Fixed price contract with variable scope.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any scope addition without a corresponding price increase produces silent budget erosion that neither party acknowledges until it becomes a dispute.

failure-mode.description: Single-point estimates with no uncertainty range.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Empirically, software estimates are systematically optimistic by 50-100%. Without a pessimistic case, the cost baseline has no resilience to the most-likely outcome.

failure-mode.description: Re-estimating but not re-baselining after a scope change.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The original baseline remains the reference; EVM metrics compare against it even though scope changed, making all variance numbers meaningless.
